info.title: Maestro Personal API
info.description: HTTP API for Maestro Personal v1 — wraps the PersonalShell (Core via Python)

get.description:
Health check — no auth required. Returns deterministic build identity.

Uses JSONResponse with Cache-Control: no-store to prevent Railway's
edge proxy from caching the response and serving stale version strings.

post.description:
Purge all demo_seed-sourced signals from ALL users.

P1 PERMANENT FIX: the product is now a real-data pilot. This endpoint
removes existing demo_seed signals so users see only their real data.

Governance: scoped strictly to metadata LIKE '%demo_seed%'. Real user
data (Gmail-sourced) is NEVER touched. The action is logged.

Auth: requires MAESTRO_PERSONAL_TOKEN (admin-level, not user-level).

get.description:
Purge all demo_seed-sourced signals. GET for easy curl testing.

Query params:
    token: MAESTRO_PERSONAL_TOKEN (admin auth)
    dry_run: if "1", report only without deleting

FORENSIC-002 P0 FIX: migrate dev:base64 tokens to Fernet encryption.

Reads all stored connector tokens, re-encrypts any that start with 'dev:'
(old format) to Fernet (new format). Transition-safe: _decrypt() handles
both formats, so this can run without breaking existing connections.

Auth: requires MAESTRO_PERSONAL_TOKEN (admin-level).

post.description:
[C] Critic contradiction probe — feed the critic a denial-while-evidence-
contains-it case and verify the critic catches it.

The auditor's [C] gap: the ask_critic was wired but never tested against
a real denial-while-evidence-contains-it case. This endpoint:
  1. Accepts {answer, query, evidence_texts: [...]} as JSON
  2. Runs ask_critic.evaluate_answer() on the triple
  3. Returns {score, justification, suggestions}

A passing probe: an answer that DENIES the commitment while the evidence
clearly contains it should score <0.5 (the critic catches the
contradiction).

Auth: requires MAESTRO_PERSONAL_TOKEN (admin-level). Same gate as
/api/admin/purge-demo-data and /api/admin/migrate-encryption.

Re-classify ALL existing signals with the current classifier.

Auditor Principle 5: "A classifier change re-classifies the data. The
classifier code is fixed (the gold-set proves it), but the existing
ledger entries were classified by the old classifier and never
re-classified. A classifier change must trigger a migration over
existing data, or the fix is only forward-looking and history stays wrong."

This endpoint:
  1. Fetches all signals from the DB
  2. Re-runs _rule_based_classify on each signal's text
  3. Updates the signal_type + metadata with the new classification
  4. Returns a report of what changed

Auth: requires admin token (MAESTRO_PERSONAL_TOKEN).
Login — returns a bearer token.
Register a new account with email + password.
Revoke the current token (P1-4 fix).
Rotate the current token (P1-4 fix).

Store the user's Expo push token for sending notifications.

Issue 6: Called by the mobile app on login to register the device
for push notifications. The notification_scheduler uses these tokens
to send stale-commitment alerts.
Ask a question — get the truth, sourced (LLM-powered when available).
Streaming Ask — SSE for sub-2s perceived latency.

get.description:
Get active commitments — calls Core's commitment classifier via the shell.

DEPTH: each commitment includes calibration_note (from CalibrationPrimitives)
and outcome_history (from BehavioralLearningEngine).

schema.title: Response Get Commitments Api Commitments Get

get.description: The one commitment at risk today — not a list of 47.
Read the normalized commitments ledger.

post.description: Transition a commitment to a new lifecycle state.

Simulate the impact of taking on a new commitment.

Directive 4: 'If I take this on, what conflicts with my existing
commitments?' Analyzes deadline overlaps, entity overload, topic
conflicts, and priority dilution.

get.description: Get all detected situations from personal signals.

schema.title: Response Get Situations Api Situations Get

post.description: Create a new personal signal (manual entry for v1).

get.description:
Get all stored signals (scoped to the authenticated user).

P3 fix: returns classification metadata (commitment_type, is_commitment,
classification_reasoning, llm_powered) so the user and auditor can see
WHY each signal was classified as a commitment. Inspectable memory.

schema.title: Response Get Signals Api Signals Get

post.description: Correct or dismiss a signal (F7 fix).

Sync Gmail messages → signals.

Accepts pre-fetched Gmail messages (the OAuth wiring happens in the
mobile app or a background worker). Extracts commitments, follow-ups,
and meeting changes using the Gmail adapter.

Sync Calendar events → signals.

Accepts pre-fetched calendar events. Extracts meeting.scheduled,
meeting.cancelled, and deadline.approaching signals.

Ingest Slack messages and extract commitments.

Directive 3: expand data sources beyond Gmail/Calendar.
Parses Slack messages, extracts commitments using the commitment
classifier, and stores them as signals.

Ingest a voice transcript and extract commitments.

Directive 3: extract implicit commitments from voice transcripts.
Processes transcript chunks, extracts commitments using voice-specific
patterns + commitment classifier, and stores them as signals.

get.description: List all available connectors with the user's connection state.

post.description: Connect a provider (stores OAuth token encrypted).

get.description:
Gmail OAuth2 callback — exchanges authorization code for tokens.

Also handles Calendar OAuth callbacks (single-callback pattern).
When state contains 'connector=calendar', dispatches to Calendar handling
instead of Gmail. This avoids needing a separate redirect URI for Calendar.
Slack OAuth2 callback — exchanges authorization code for tokens.
Google Calendar OAuth2 callback — exchanges authorization code for tokens.
GitHub OAuth2 callback — exchanges authorization code for tokens.
Yahoo Mail OAuth2 callback — exchanges authorization code for tokens.

Microsoft Mail OAuth2 callback — exchanges authorization code for tokens.

The admin_consent query param is set to 'True' when an enterprise admin
pre-approves the scopes for their tenant. We accept it as a string
(Microsoft sends it as a query param) and surface it in the response
so the UI can show "admin consent granted" messaging.

delete.description: Disconnect a provider (deletes the token, keeps audit history).

post.description: Pull messages from a connector and ingest commitments as signals.

get.description: Get the connector + draft audit log for the current user.

post.description: Create a pending draft for user approval.

get.description: List drafts for the current user, optionally filtered by status.

post.description:
DERIVE a draft from the user's signal history — the real capability (P13 fix).

Only takes provider + recipient. Maestro DERIVES:
  1. The commitment (by searching the user's signals for commitments to this recipient)
  2. The evidence_refs (via keyword match + FTS5 retrieval on the recipient name)

If no commitments are found for the recipient, returns 404 with guidance.

get.description: Get a single draft by ID.

post.description:
Resolve a draft: approve (send), deny (discard), or use_draft (open in compose).

The approval flow is the trust mechanism — Maestro NEVER auto-sends.
Every draft requires explicit human approval.

delete.description: Delete the user's account and all associated data (scoped to caller).

get.description: Export all user data (GDPR/CCPA compliance).

post.description: Register a device for push notifications.
Deliver high-priority whispers as push notifications.

get.description: List all wired Nerve agents.
Unified dashboard view: all insights from all agents.
Query a specific agent's insights.
Get dynamically selected agents for a situation (Directive 4).

post.description: Register a prediction — the START of the learning loop.
Resolve a prediction with the actual outcome — CLOSES the learning loop.

get.description: Get the Brier score + calibration report.
Get calibration history — Brier score trends over time.
Get the current processing mode for privacy transparency.

Get the data retention TTL configuration (Step 15).

Returns the enforced retention periods for each data type, so users
can see exactly how long their data is kept.
Get per-connector consent toggles for the current user.

put.description:
Update per-connector consent toggles for the current user.

Body: {"provider": "gmail", "scope": "create_drafts", "enabled": false}

get.description: Get the audit log — every data access event.
Verify whether the Cognitive Council is LLM-powered or rule-based.
TEMP debug — inspect LLM router state.
Show which Core modules are wired to Personal.
Get the personal knowledge graph summary for an entity.
Get the risk prediction for a new commitment with this entity.
Get the user's behavior patterns for personalization.
Get all events for a trace ID.
Get recent traces for the authenticated user.
Get recent whisper decisions for the authenticated user.
Get success metrics — tracks real user value.
Get ambient intelligence — what's happening between calls.
Verify situation persistence across restart (S2 beta blocker fix).
Get recent meaningful deltas.

schema.title: Response Get What Changed Api What Changed Get

get.description: The 2 things that materially shifted — not a feed.
Get preparation for upcoming situations — 3 things that matter.

schema.title: Response Get Prepare Api Prepare Get

get.description:
Get active whispers — things that deserve attention RIGHT NOW.

DEPTH: calls Core's WhisperSituationBridge.from_situation() for each
situation. Empty list = trusted silence.

schema.title: Response Get Whispers Api Whisper Get

get.description: Morning briefing — the full Situation-centric intelligence.
Evening briefing — what happened today, what's pending.

The single most important thing Maestro knows right now.

The Spotlight moment — the one commitment that matters most. Not a list.
If nothing deserves attention, returns has_moment=False.

post.description: Get context-aware ambient notifications for the current user.
Get calendar awareness for upcoming meetings.

get.description: Get commitment escalations for the current user.

post.description: Get cross-meeting threads linking related meetings by entity + topic.

get.description:
Get cross-meeting threads for a specific entity.

Convenience endpoint — used by /api/ask to surface the thread when the
user asks about an entity. Returns the same shape as POST /threads but
filtered to the specified entity.

Get the decision history for an entity across meetings.

Surfaces the "Decided to offer phased rollout (Oct 22); confirmed in
Nov 5 call" capability — decisions tracked across meetings as a chain.
Get grades for all meetings for the current user.

Get the grade for a specific meeting.

Returns 404 if the meeting signal isn't found in the user's history.

post.description: Override the computed grade for a meeting.

get.description: Get deal health scores for all entities.

Get the deal health score for a specific entity.

Returns 404 if the entity has no signals.
Get the organizational learning report.

Get a one-line flywheel summary for the user.

Convenience endpoint — useful for dashboard headers + mobile UI.
List all 20 synthetic emails.

post.description: Receive a synthetic email — ingests it as a signal, triggering commitment extraction.

get.description: Summary of synthetic emails received + their effects on commitments.

AskResponse.title: AskResponse
AskResponse.description: The masterpiece Ask response — the truth, sourced, with full depth.

BriefingResponse.title: BriefingResponse
BriefingResponse.description: The masterpiece briefing — Situation-centric, not agent-centric.

CalendarAwarenessRequest.title: CalendarAwarenessRequest
CalendarAwarenessRequest.description:
Request body for /calendar/awareness.

P13: caller supplies only a time horizon (CONTEXT), not meeting data.
The meeting context is DERIVED from the user's signal history.

CommitmentsMasterpieceResponse.title: CommitmentsMasterpieceResponse
CommitmentsMasterpieceResponse.description:
The masterpiece Commitments response — one at risk, rest secondary.

Not a list of 47. One primary (the at-risk commitment), the rest
available but secondary. The inevitability: you know what you owe
without scrolling.

ConnectorAutoDraftRequest.title: ConnectorAutoDraftRequest
ConnectorAutoDraftRequest.description: P13 fix: only provider + recipient — commitment + evidence are DERIVED.

MeetingOverrideRequest.title: MeetingOverrideRequest
MeetingOverrideRequest.description:
Request body for overriding a meeting grade.

P13: the caller supplies the grade letter (user judgment), not the
meeting data. The meeting data is DERIVED from signal history.

PrepareResponse.title: PrepareResponse
PrepareResponse.description:
The masterpiece Prepare response — 3 things that matter for THIS meeting.

Not 5 prep points. Three. The forgotten commitment, the open question,
the contradiction. The right three. PLUS: Cluely-class depth from
CopilotSituationBridge.pre_call_briefing().

SmartNotificationRequest.title: SmartNotificationRequest
SmartNotificationRequest.description:
Request body for /notifications/smart.

Per P13: the caller supplies CONTEXT (am I in a call? is DND on?),
NOT the notification content. The notifications themselves are DERIVED
from the user's signal history by the AmbientNotificationEngine.

TheMomentResponse.title: TheMomentResponse
TheMomentResponse.description: The single most important thing Maestro knows right now.

ThreadRequest.title: ThreadRequest
ThreadRequest.description:
Request body for /threads.

P13: caller supplies only an optional entity filter (CONTEXT), not
meeting data. The threads are DERIVED from the user's signal history.

WhatChangedMasterpieceResponse.title: WhatChangedMasterpieceResponse
WhatChangedMasterpieceResponse.description:
The masterpiece What Changed response — 2 material shifts, not a feed.

Not a chronological inbox dump. Two cards. The things that materially
changed since you last looked. The inevitability: you're already
caught up.
